title: Caramelized Sugar Poached Bean Stir-fry
description: A fresh and tender crowd-pleasing Slovakian dish featuring a delightful caramelized sugar sauce that complements the poached beans, making it both visually stunning and deliciously comforting.
instructions: Rinse the beans and pick out any debris. Soak them in water for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a pan with oil over medium heat, add garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour the soaked beans into the pan along with enough water to cover them by half. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Simmer for about 20 minutes or until the beans are tender but still hold their shape.

While the beans are cooking, in another pan, combine sugar and salt over low heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Once dissolved, add a pinch of red pepper flakes if using, and cook for about 5-7 minutes to achieve desired caramelization level.
Taste the caramel sauce before pouring it over the poached beans. Adjust with more salt or sugar as needed.
Gently fold the bean mixture into the caramelized sauce until coated evenly.
Serve hot as a side dish or on its own for a quick and delicious snack.
